economic_notes: Investments in MERE General Partner Inc. (modified equity), HIAH Economic Dev LP (99.99% interest), U.C.C.M. Building Material Supply LP (14.28%), M'Nidoo M'Nising LP (16.55%), Robinson Huron Forestry Inc. (12.5%), Great Lakes Hospitality Group Partnership (29.44%). HIAH engaged in business ventures for general welfare and development of M'Chigeeng First Nation.
notes: MERE General Partner Inc. and HIAH Economic Dev LP accounted for by modified equity method (not fully consolidated). Deferred revenue increased significantly from $4.79M to $10.80M, primarily from Indigenous Services Canada ($9.38M). HIAH reported a net loss of $147,395 for the year. Advances to MERE ($3.8M) are unsecured, non-interest bearing with no repayment terms. Health expenses combined Health Services ($1,960,416) and Health Agreement ($2,050,117). Documents provided are pages 1-10; remuneration schedule not included in provided pages.
